snippet.title: 05-02-23 (rerun) Dr. R. Leo Sprinkle, Decades of UFO & Abductee Research
snippet.description:
To become a Supporter: https://www.patreon.com/MartinWillisPodcastUFO  Rerun (original April 9, 2015) guest Dr. Leo Sprinkle talked about his two sightings, his vast career and the thousands of cases he had looked into. He also talked about free energy, abductees, J Allen Hynek and more. 

BIO: Dr R. Leo Sprinkle (1930-2021) psychologist and researcher of UFO contactee experiences, was born on August 31, 1930, in Rocky Ford, Colorado. He received his bachelor's and master's degrees from the University of Colorado and in 1961 completed his doctorate in counseling psychology at the University of Missouri. After three years in the administrative department at the University of North Dakota, he moved to the University of Wyoming, where he remained until his retirement in 1989.

Sprinkle has traced his interest in UFOs to a sighting he had in 1949. He and his wife also had a sighting in 1956. During the years immediately following the completion of his formal education, he conducted several studies, including an early survey of the members of the National Investigations Committee on Aerial Phenomena and an initial study of people who had experienced extraterrestrial encounters, both contactees and abductees. He served as a psychological consultant for the Condon Report (1969) on UFOs, which led to further work on several abduction cases through the 1970s.

Sprinkle is best known, however, for the annual conferences he has organized for UFO contactees each summer since in 1980. For a number of years, Sprinkle had corresponded with people who had claimed friendly contact with the entities who drove the flying saucers. Unlike most ufologists, he had not dismissed them; in contrast, he had responded to them sympathetically. The conferences, sponsored by the Institute for UFO Contactee Studies, brought contactees to the university and provided an open forum for them to tell their stories in a nonjudgmental environment. As the number of abduction reports increased in the late 1980s, abductees were welcomed to the summer conferences and it was in these conferences that the sharp distinction between the two groups began to disappear.

Eventually, Sprinkle identified himself as a contactee. He also concluded that UFO activity was part of a larger program of what he termed "cosmic consciousness conditioning." The UFO entities, whether one thinks of them as being from outer space or another dimension, are attempting to move humanity into an understanding of themselves as cosmic citizens.

Podcast UFO was established November, 2011. We live-stream here on YouTube every Tuesday Evening 7PM ET (New York time) and discuss UFOs/UAPs and other varying related topics. 
Check out our website: https://podcastufo.com/ 
Please Support us on Patreon for $2 or more per month: https://www.patreon.com/MartinWillisPodcastUFO
Show Notes for all of our shows: https://podcastufo.com/show-notes/
Follow us on Twitter: https://twitter.com/PodcastUFO
Our Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/PodcastUFO
Host, Martin Willis: The subject of UFOs has fascinated Martin Willis since his youth, yet he was on the fence about the subject. He experienced a personal sighting in 2007 in Carmel Valley, CA, that solidified his beliefs that Unidentifiable Flying Objects really do exist. Since this time, Martin has paid attention to what others have to say about the phenomenon with some skepticism, yet an open mind. Pondering what UFOs may actually be, pushed his interest to another level. Martin thinks we may never find out what UFOs/UAP are, but it  is the most important and exciting puzzle yet to solve. Martin has an interest in astronomy, feels we are not alone and always maintained a strong scientific fascination of our vast universe. As a UFO enthusiast, Martin created Podcast UFO in November, 2011 with over 500 show interviews. Martin was the keynote speaker at the Pine Bush, UFO Conference in 2022. Martin is a long time professional antique & fine art appraiser.   https://seaboardappraisals.com/ Martin began podcasting on antiques, auctions and art in 2009 https://antiqueauctionforum.com/
#ufotwitter #uap

snippet.title: Shocking Ways AI Could End The World  - Geoffrey Miller
snippet.description:
Geoffrey Miller is a professor of evolutionary psychology at the University of New Mexico, a researcher and an author.

Artificial Intelligence possesses the capability to process information thousands of times faster than humans. It's opened up massive possibilities. But it's also opened up huge debate about the safety of creating a machine which is more intelligent and powerful than we are. Just how legitimate are the concerns about the future of AI?

snippet.title: Keith Milward's Showbiz Show with Scott Jones
snippet.description:
Keith Milward talks to Scott Jones about the Stars Musical Theatre Company's production of The Phantom Of The Opera. This is a unique performance as only four licences to produce the show was issued, one in each of Wales, England, Ireland and Scotland  by arrangement of the really useful group ltd. 
Based on the 1910 French novel of the same name, Phantom of the Opera, tells the story of beautiful soprano, Christine Daaé, who becomes the obsession of a mysterious, masked musical genius living in the subterranean labyrinth beneath the Paris Opéra House. 
The romantic, haunting and soaring score includes Music of the Night, All I Ask of You, Wishing You Were Somehow Here Again, Masquerade and the iconic title song. 
Experience Andrew Lloyd Webber's most haunting love story and one of the most successful musicals of all time, PHANTOM OF THE OPERA as this legendary show is brought to life at Gwyn Hall Neath by Stars Musical Theatre Company.

snippet.title: Highlights - ICoCA AGA 2022 Day 3 Panel - Sports and Private Security
snippet.description:
The full discussion can be viewed here: https://youtu.be/7yLu7eRsIxo

Private security plays a critical role in securing sporting events around the world. From providing stewards and guarding venues, to protecting the athletes themselves, the demand for private security providers at some of the world’s largest sporting events can be enormous. So what are the human rights risks that sports take for the organisation of mega sporting events, and what can they do to minimize these risks?

With the Qatar 2022 World Cup in full swing, our expert panel unpacked some of the issues at play as we consider what’s at stake when mega sporting events, private security and human rights interact.

Panelists:
- Guido Battaglia, Head of Policy & Outreach, Centre for Sport and Human Rights
- Michael Leahy, Policy & Strategic Projects, FIFPRO
- Ronan Evain, Executive Director, Football Supporters Europe
- Rémy Friedmann, Senior Advisor, Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, Government of Switzerland
- Magali Martowicz, Head of Human Rights, International Olympic Committee

Moderator: Nuala Walsh, CEO, MindEquity

Date: 7 December 2022

This session was organised in partnership with the Center for Sport and Human Rights.

snippet.title: Dolores Keane & Emmet Cahill - Where Have All The Flowers Gone | Fleadh 2023 | TG4
snippet.description:
Féach siar ar Fleadh 2023 anseo ~ Watch it back here
▶️ https://nasc.tg4.tv/Fleadh2023

Tá Doireann Ní Ghlacáin, Peadar Ó Goill agus Aoife Ní Thuairisg sa Mhuileann gCearr d'Fhleadh Cheoil na hÉireann 2023, an fhéile is mó sa domhan ó thaobh cheol, amhráin agus dhamhsa traidisiúnta na hÉireann. Beidh neart craic agus ceol ag teacht beo ón mbaile a raibh an chéad Fhleadh ar siúl ann in 1951 agus beidh daoine mór le rá chomh maith le seaimpíní nua na hÉireann le feiceáil feadh cheithre oíche i ndiaidh a chéile.

Doireann Ní Ghlacáin, Peadar Ó Goill and Aoife Ní Thuairisg land in Mullingar for Fleadh Cheoil na hÉireann 2023, the world's largest festival of Irish music, song and dance. From well-known names to newly crowned All-Ireland champions, audiences will be treated to four consecutive nights of live entertainment coming from the town where the very first Fleadh Cheoil was held in 1951.

snippet.title: Sue Johnson, Giulia Altera e Andrea Pagani per EFT Italia - Nuove Traduzioni EFT in Italiano
snippet.description:
🇮🇹 "In un incontro emozionante nella casa di Sue Johnson a Victoria, Canada, Giulia Altera e Andrea Pagani di EFT Italia hanno condiviso una chiacchierata intensa ed emotiva con la fondatrice dell'Emotionally Focused Therapy. Questo speciale momento ci ha permesso di esplorare temi profondi, come la solitudine del terapeuta, il potere della connessione emotiva nella terapia di coppia e le sfide e le opportunità offerte dalla Terapia Individuale Focalizzata sulle Emozioni (EFIT).

Siamo orgogliosi di presentare i due nuovi testi tradotti in italiano: 'Stringimi Forte' e la 'Guida per la Terapia Individuale Focalizzata sulle Emozioni', strumenti preziosi per terapeuti e appassionati di EFT che desiderano approfondire e arricchire il proprio lavoro e la propria comprensione delle dinamiche emotive.

Questa conversazione, ricca di riflessioni e momenti toccanti, rappresenta un'opportunità unica per entrare nel cuore dell'EFT e scoprire come questo approccio possa trasformare le relazioni e il modo in cui ci connettiamo con noi stessi e con gli altri.

Scopri di più sulle traduzioni e sull’EFT in Italia:
📧 Email: info@eftitaliacommunity.com
🌐 www.eftitaliacommunity.com
📸 Seguici su Instagram: @giulia_altera e @eftitalia

🇬🇧 "In a heartfelt meeting at Sue Johnson's home in Victoria, Canada, Giulia Altera and Andrea Pagani from EFT Italia engaged in an intense and moving conversation with the founder of Emotionally Focused Therapy. This special moment allowed us to delve into profound topics such as the therapist's solitude, the power of emotional connection in couple therapy, and the challenges and opportunities of Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy (EFIT).

We are proud to present two new Italian translations: 'Hold Me Tight' and the 'Guide for Emotionally Focused Individual Therapy'. These are invaluable tools for therapists and EFT enthusiasts who seek to deepen their work and understanding of emotional dynamics.

Christine Diane Teigen aka Chrissy Teigen was born on November 30, 1985 and is an American model and television personality. Her debut as a professional model was in the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue in 2010, and she later graced the 50th anniversary cover alongside Nina Agdal and Lily Aldridge in 2014. She was previously a panellist on the syndicated daytime talk show FABLife (2015–2016). Teigen co-hosted Lip Sync Battle (2015–2019), a musical competition series with LL Cool J, and was a judge on Bring the Funny (2019), a comedy competition series. In addition to her television work, Teigen is also an author of three cookbooks.

She is 5 feet 8 inches tall and weighs 55 kgs or 121 pounds. Her body measurements are 36-28-34.

With an annual income of $39 million and an estimated net worth of $75 million, Christine Teigen is one of the highest-paid models.

During the pilot and first season of the game show Deal or No Deal, Teigen worked as a briefcase model. Her appearance in the 2010 Sports Illustrated swimsuit issue led to her being named "Rookie of the Year," thanks in part to her friend and fellow model Brooklyn Decker, who had introduced her to the casting team. In 2011, Chrissy Teigen debuted a capsule collection with swimwear designer DiNeila Brazil at Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Swim in Miami, and she also appeared in the Electronic Arts video game Need For Speed: The Run. Additionally, she filmed a special for the Cooking Channel called Cookies and Cocktails.
Chrissy Teigen later hosted the competition series Model Employee on VH1 and was featured on another Cooking Channel special called Chrissy Teigen's Hungry, which chronicled her wedding menu tasting with her then-fiancé, John Legend. In October 2013, she appeared in Legend's music video for the song "All of Me," which included footage from their wedding. She played a fictionalized version of herself as a relationship counsellor in an Inside Amy Schumer sketch in April 2014. That same year, Teigen appeared on the 50th anniversary cover of the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Issue with Nina Agdal and Lily Aldridge. In January 2015, she made a guest appearance on the sitcom The Mindy Project as the girlfriend of the main character's first sexual partner. She became a co-host of the musical competition series Lip Sync Battle alongside LL Cool J in April of the same year and co-hosted the 2015 Billboard Music Awards with Ludacris.

Rosie Huntington-Whiteley is an English model and actress born on 18 April 1987 in Plymouth, Devon, England.

She is the daughter of Fiona, a fitness instructor, and Charles, a chartered surveyor, she has a brother and a sister.

Rosie Huntington’s Net Worth: $30 Million
Rosie Huntington’s Height: 5 ft 8 in (1.75 m)

She is best known for her work for lingerie retailer Victoria’s Secret, formerly being one of their brand “Angels”, for being the face of Burberry’s 2011 brand fragrance “Burberry Body”, for her work with Marks & Spencer, and, most recently, for her artistic collaboration with denim-focused fashion brand Paige.

Moving into acting, she had supporting roles as Carly Spencer in the 2011 film Transformers: Dark of the Moon, the third installment in the Transformers film series, and as The Splendid Angharad in the 2015 film Mad Max: Fury Road.

While a 15-year-old student, she undertook work experience at London West End-based model agency Profile, before taking her GCSEs.
Signed after leaving school in summer 2003 aged 16, her first modelling session was for a Levi’s jeans commercial.
She got her first big break in January 2004 when she travelled to New York City for a shoot with Teen Vogue.
Rosie later made her catwalk début alongside Naomi Campbell in New York City in Spring 2004.

In early 2006, she was signed to American lingerie brand Victoria’s Secret, making her début at the brand’s fashion show in Los Angeles.
Since 2010, she has been in a relationship with actor Jason Statham.
In January 2016, her representative confirmed they had become engaged.
Their son Jack Oscar Statham was born on 24 June 2017.

Rosie Huntington-Whiteley is an English model and actress born on 18 April 1987 in Plymouth, Devon, England.

She is the daughter of Fiona, a fitness instructor, and Charles, a chartered surveyor, she has a brother and a sister.

Rosie Huntington’s Net Worth: $30 Million
Rosie Huntington’s Height: 5 ft 8 in (1.75 m)

She is best known for her work for lingerie retailer Victoria’s Secret, formerly being one of their brand “Angels”, for being the face of Burberry’s 2011 brand fragrance “Burberry Body”, for her work with Marks & Spencer, and, most recently, for her artistic collaboration with denim-focused fashion brand Paige.

Moving into acting, she had supporting roles as Carly Spencer in the 2011 film Transformers: Dark of the Moon, the third installment in the Transformers film series, and as The Splendid Angharad in the 2015 film Mad Max: Fury Road.

While a 15-year-old student, she undertook work experience at London West End-based model agency Profile, before taking her GCSEs.
Signed after leaving school in summer 2003 aged 16, her first modelling session was for a Levi’s jeans commercial.
She got her first big break in January 2004 when she travelled to New York City for a shoot with Teen Vogue.
Rosie later made her catwalk début alongside Naomi Campbell in New York City in Spring 2004.

In early 2006, she was signed to American lingerie brand Victoria’s Secret, making her début at the brand’s fashion show in Los Angeles.
Since 2010, she has been in a relationship with actor Jason Statham.
In January 2016, her representative confirmed they had become engaged.
Their son Jack Oscar Statham was born on 24 June 2017.

Gisele Bündchen, born on July 20, 1980, in Horizontina, Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil, is a Brazilian model who gained fame in the late 1990s and became a "supermodel." She is well known as a face of Victoria's Secret, an American lingerie, clothing, and beauty retailer. Her net worth in 2023 is valued at $400 million, and her annual income is $40 million.

Bündchen grew up in Horizontina, a small rural town in southern Brazil, with her five sisters, including her fraternal twin. Her father was a university teacher, and her mother worked as a bank clerk.
In 1994, at the age of 14, Bündchen was discovered by a modeling agent for Elite Model Management while shopping at a mall. She participated in the agency's Look of the Year modeling contest (later renamed Elite Model Look) in 1995 and placed second nationally and fourth globally. The following year, she moved to New York City to start her professional modeling career and made her runway debut at New York Fashion Week. Despite her more curvaceous figure, which contrasted with the waifish frames then in style, Bündchen continued to appear on the covers of the leading fashion magazines. In 1999, she was named Model of the Year, a distinction awarded jointly by Vogue and the American cable-television network VH1.

In 2000, Bündchen signed a contract to become a face of Victoria's Secret (2000–07) and gained international notoriety after starting a relationship with Hollywood actor Leonardo DiCaprio. She soon became a household name and was recognized as a "supermodel." During this time, Bündchen also walked for top fashion houses, such as Christian Dior and Valentino, until her retirement from runway modeling in 2015.

Bündchen made her film debut in the comedic thriller Taxi (2004), alongside Jimmy Fallon and Queen Latifah. Two years later, she starred in the film adaptation of Lauren Weisberger's best-selling novel The Devil Wears Prada (2004), with Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway.
In 2009, Bündchen married American football player Tom Brady and was appointed Goodwill Ambassador for the United Nations Environment Programme. She launched several product lines, including footwear, clothing, jewelry, lingerie, and environmentally friendly skin care. Bündchen founded the Luz Foundation, which supports various self-esteem-building programs for young women. In 2018, she published Lessons: My Path to a Meaningful Life. In 2009, Bündchen was featured in the New York Metropolitan Museum of Art exhibition "The Model as Muse: Embodying Fashion," which highlighted the models who epitomized fashion during the 20th century.

Isabella Khair Hadid, popularly known as Bella Hadid, is an American model and actress with a net worth of $25 million as of 2023. She has been active in the industry since 2012 and is represented by the agency IMG Models. In 2016, she was named "Model of the Year" by industry professionals on Models.com. Hadid has also expressed interest in pursuing acting.
As of 2023, Hadid is 26 years old, having been born on October 9, 1996. She grew up in a wealthy Christian family in Washington D.C., United States, but now identifies as Muslim. She attended Malibu High School in Washington D.C. before graduating from Parsons School of Design in New York with a degree in fashion photography. Hadid was more interested in sports and extracurricular activities during her childhood than in academics.
Hadid's parents are Mohamed and Yolanda Hadid. Her father is a real estate developer, while her mother is a TV personality and former model. Hadid has four siblings, including Gigi Hadid, Alana Hadid, Mariella Hadid, and Anwar Hadid, who is also a model.
Hadid is currently unmarried but is in a relationship with Marc Kalman, an art director. Prior to Kalman, she has dated several notable figures, including Matthew Morton, Stella Maxwell, Odell Beckham Jr, Anthony Joshua, and Jordan Clarkson.
She stands at 5 feet and 9 inches tall, weighs around 54 kg, and has a figure measuring approximately 34-24-34 inches. Hadid's long hair is ash blonde, and her eyes are a beautiful blue-green color.

Anastasia Kvitko, born on November 25, 1994, is a Russian glamour model and entrepreneur who gained popularity for her striking figure. Kvitko grew up in western Russia, in the Kaliningrad Oblast region, before moving to the United States as a teenager to pursue her dream of becoming a model.

Initially, Kvitko landed in Miami, hoping to kickstart her modeling career. However, despite her stunning figure, she was turned down by several modeling agencies for not fitting the conventional body type. Nevertheless, Kvitko refused to give up and instead used her unique curves to her advantage, developing a brand that celebrated her unique physical features.

Eventually, Kvitko moved to Los Angeles to focus on modeling full-time, where she gained significant recognition for her Instagram presence. She has amassed a huge following on the platform, with over 12.2 million followers as of 2017. Due to her resemblance to Kim Kardashian, she was dubbed the "Russian Kim Kardashian," which helped her rise to fame.

However, her fame has also brought criticism from many, with some accusing her of copying Kardashian's signature style and even undergoing cosmetic surgery to emulate her. Despite the comparisons, Kvitko has stated in interviews that she admires Kardashian but does not appreciate being compared to her. She has also expressed a desire to surpass Kardashian's success in the future.

Apart from modeling, Kvitko is also an entrepreneur, having launched her own swimsuit line, which has gained a significant following. Her success has made her an inspiration for many young women who do not fit into the traditional mold of a model, and she continues to inspire and motivate others to embrace their unique features and pursue their dreams.
